steps.title: Identify slope and y-intercept
steps.content: We are given the slope $m = \frac{1}{4}$ and that the line passes through $(0, 5)$. Since this point has $x = 0$, it lies on the $y$-axis and represents the $y$-intercept: $b = 5$.

steps.title: Write the equation using slope-intercept form
steps.content:
Substituting $m = \frac{1}{4}$ and $b = 5$ into the general form:
$$f(x) = \frac{1}{4}x + 5$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
